title: Inflation rate rises to 11.61 percent in October - FinancialEDGE
description: The National Bureau of Statistics on Monday November 18, 2019, released the Consumer Price Index which measures inflation with the index rising to 11.61 per cent in October. The NBS in its report said the 11.61 per cent is 0.36 percentage points higher than the 11.24 per cent recorded in September. The rise in inflation
html:
The National Bureau of Statistics on Monday November 18, 2019, released the Consumer Price Index which measures inflation with the index rising to 11.61 per cent in October. The NBS in its report said the 11.61 per cent is 0.36 percentage points higher than the 11.24 per cent recorded in September.

The rise in inflation rate according to experts may be connected to the ongoing border closure which had made it difficult for products to be exported and imported into the country through the land borders

The report reads in part, “The consumer price index, which measures inflation rose by 11.61 per cent year-on-year in October 2019. “This is 0.36 percentage points higher than the rate recorded in September 2019 (11.24 percent).” On month-on-month basis, the report said the index increased by 1.07 per cent in October, adding that this is 0.03 percentage points higher than the 1.04 per cent recorded in September.
